unit.note: Full fresh suites at F0: shared 3934 passed / 13 skipped; shipwright-grade 392 passed / 6 skipped; shipwright-adopt 379 passed / 1 skipped. ruff@0.15.15 clean. The 2 gate skips are the BOOTSTRAP arm (origin/main publishes no contract fixture yet — this is the commit that introduces one); they assert `not any_published_contract` first, so a renamed CONTRACTS_DIR cannot disarm the gate by masquerading as a bootstrap.

e2e.note: CLI surface: scripts/verify_contract_surface.py drives BOTH producers end-to-end — `grade.py --format json` and `analyze_codebase.py` — against a real repository and validates the emitted bytes against the contract fixture published for the version they claim. Both conform.

surface_verification.justification: The user-facing surface of this change IS the two artifacts a consumer fetches. The runner invokes each producer's real CLI and checks the real output — a unit pin over the dataclass would not catch a wrapper or encoder silently changing what reaches the Command Center.

degraded.detail: classify_complexity emitted `touches_migrations` from the word 'schema' in the run description. Prose-only: this repo has no migrations, and the diff touches none. Overridden; the diff-derived predicates in risk_detectors.py are authoritative and F11 recomputes them. The real flag is touches_io_boundary (both artifacts are serialized producer/consumer boundaries), which was honored — round-trip / boundary verification is exactly what the contract gates and the F0.5 CLI runner do.

behaviors.behavior: a shape change with no version bump FAILS
behaviors.evidence: TestRequireBump; TestEndToEndGate (against a real git baseline)

behaviors.behavior: performed >= required, not equality (bundling + semantic hatch)
EDITING a published fixture is caught (the load-bearing half)
DELETING a published fixture is caught
bootstrap stand-down cannot be abused by renaming the dir
a container BECOMING NULLABLE demands a major (adds no field, but the consumer indexes it)
weak pins rejected: empty arrays and null-only leaves
the pin describes the SERIALIZED artifact, not just the dataclass
status vocabulary probed against the REAL engine, not the test fixture's re-implementation
both SKILLs state the contract with substance (consumer named, rule stated, version semantics, frozen baseline)
schema_version is ADDITIVE — a snapshot written before it existed stays readable; no reader may require it
git_analyzer detects major_refactor_commits at all (it returned [] for every repo — dead field)
a '|' in a commit subject does not shift date/author
terminal/markdown/HTML renders are byte-unchanged by the new field
behaviors.evidence: test_render_snapshots.py goldens pass unchanged — the new field is machine metadata; adding it changed no rendered row, and the existing golden IS the assertion.
